There is no god in generic sense people make wrt to religious beliefs
But i believe there is sure a superpower that we dont know about

Same with the concept of god

If we can imagine it then its not god
If there is a superpower then it must be beyond our imaginaton otherwise there is no difference between the god and our imagination

THIS IS MY OPINION IRRESPECTIVE OF ANYTHING
I RESPECT YOURS😊
Its a product of our imagination
But there sure is some superpower which we cannot define
Rest krishna, jesus, allah are just imaginations

But i didnt define anything

Like if i dont draw a painting
I cant fall in love with the blank paper
There are proofs of lord rama too and there is an actual bridge between india and srilanka made up of stones
Does that mean every hindu mythology is true?

Like think about ghosts like bloody mary or etc.
Someone drew it on paper or animated it or created a story
So when you fear the ghost
You arent fearing the literal ghost 
You are fearing the image you create about the ghost

If lets say the ghost were drawn like a cute bunny
Would anyone fear it?
No

Ghost was about fear

And just reverse and think yourself wrt to god and love

Umm its a 48km bridge
Created of only floating stones
It is said that

Nal and neel were 2 monkeys who had the ability to make the stones less denser and hence made them able to float on water
Geographicaly the area didnt have any such stones

So how come they build the bridge in such an ancient era

At that time they didnt have any resource

And such less densers were only found in the northern parts of india

So its essentially either a mythical story or perhaps a magical one

We dont know🤔

Like someone might believe in principles of lord buddha abt peace and calm
Its perhaps suits them

Someone perhaps believes in principles of lord narsimha like a brute force

Its instinctively what the person believes
Thats why many people change religion later in life to whatever aligns with their soul

If there is no resource and technology

Religions create a system of social well being and rules
We can argue upon it as many rules are controversial
But many are the reasons people have stability and social values

Like lets say hinduism which deals with aprox 1.5 billion people

If suddenly hinduism is banned or vanished

Suddenly the social things will collapse

There wont be any hindu festivals, or etc. As a result people's EQ will slowly decrease because apart from school and college these festivals are the only ways people socialise 

Also there may not be any god
But
Just thinking that there is someone for me calm's peoples mind and helps them pass through the struggles

And there are many other things

Everything good comes with some bad

Nuclear weapons were created to create energy and nuclear powerplants and got used for nuclear bombing instead

Internet was created for improving the tech it brought cybercrime, and scams along with it

Social media was created to connect people but it is making people more depressed and less dopamine sensitive

Does that mean we should quit all of the above things

We as humans have to take the good part instead of bad

Same with religions

Same thing different outcomes

The biggest realization a person can have in his life is that he isnt the main character, that hes not special, and he might not be able to achieve all that he dreams of. 

We all have big dreams for ourselves and we are optimistic about them, how we are going to start a startup become millionaires, and get famous. 

We need some intellectual humility and some self-reflection for our success. We are not boundless. People who ask you to chase your dreams, but are the dreams realistic? can they be achieved? 
Choose something you can actually do and have the capacity to or you might waste time. 

All the success stories are classic examples of Survivorship bias, they ask you to chase your dreams, be motivated, work hard. But there are millions of failure cases with same protocol.

Here's the lore u asked for @Raptor.og . You calling me your daughter @speed birb I see no problem in it , but i want someone to talk to 🙃 : Trying to be kind in this world where kindness isn’t understood, where negativity often rules. But I will stand with what I believe is right. I will be kind to those who are kind.
Today I realized what I truly need and want: true friends, people with character, noble qualities, morality, people who try to be better than yesterday. I knew this logically before. But now I felt it in my heart.
I met a person like that. We talked a lot. I learned about them, and they earned my respect. I never thought I would find someone like that in that place. My feelings toward them are strange. A mix of appreciation, fascination, and something deeper. The kind that makes you want to talk more, to be close, to understand them.
They have qualities I admire: honesty, kindness, doing things for others without being asked, just to make someone happier. And most importantly, innocence. The rare kind that stays pure even while being aware of the world’s negativity. The kind that doesn’t change even when the world pressures it to.
It’s sad that I can only talk to this person two times. After that, I may never see or speak to them again.
Is this love? Considering she is two years older and of the opposite gender?
I think she awakened something in me. The desire to stay good in a world that sometimes feels harsh. I always hoped I would meet someone of strong character. For me, she is the first. I’m sad I may never see her again, but I’m grateful that I met someone like her at all.
My standards are high. But even a small part of her satisfied my heart in a way I can’t explain. It’s not that she met every expectation. Maybe only 20%. Yet somehow, my heart felt content.
A 15-year-old 9th grader found something special in an 11th grader who happened to be his benchmate during exams. Life is unpredictable like that.

Logically, it may be impossible for anything more. So instead of being sad, I will choose to be grateful.
I don’t know what she feels about me. I expect nothing. But she once called me innocent.
I realized something else. I want someone to return home to someday. Someone I could live for and dedicate my efforts to. I used to live only for self-improvement. But I feel tired of living only for myself.
We talked about passions. She loves writing, novels, psychology, crafting bracelets, drawing. She even encouraged me to draw more. She has written poems on different themes. I joked once about how old writing feels cringe, and she got slightly upset, then laughed when I made fun of my past self. She explained why feeling cringe about old work actually means growth. I already knew that, but I let her explain. I liked hearing her perspective.
We talked for around 30 minutes during an exam. I might have been the only junior talking so much to a senior. And she stayed back extra time just to continue the conversation, even pretending to draw more.
She made me reflect on my current friends. Many boys in school are focused on showing off, social media, vulgar jokes. It feels shallow sometimes. Talking to her felt different. Real.
She doesn’t seem influenced by trends. She seems thoughtful about society rather than blindly following it.
It’s funny. She’s the first girl outside my family I’ve talked to this deeply. And in just two days, I spoke more openly than ever before.
She said she wants to go into business. The bracelet she made herself was beautiful, better than most market ones. When she mentioned her family is in business, I joked about “family talent,” and she laughed.
I suddenly realized something. I usually don’t go to school regularly. But the thought came that if I could talk to her, I would want to go every day.

One time i made a self deprecating joke about my hand writing and how the board guys would probably cut alot marks for it , but she took it seriously , she said it's nothing to be worried about , she told me that contents matter more , she even opened up and talk about her vulnerabilitys , like how she feels suicidal sometimes

Peoples regret when their angry hurt someone which caused an regretful thing

Peoples and empathic people discrimination on disabled people.

The sub caunsicious actions of people towards their loved ones when protecting them. 


I have seen many people regrets when they have said or did something when in an negative state which caused misfortune leading those people with even more regret.  And heavy one indeed , in an angry state people tend to say their impulses or unfiltered thoughts out loud which their rational self would be aware of .

Alot of people look down on disabled people cause they don't posses same fundamental thing as them , making them weird and odd one .
While empathetic tend to avoid them cause their empathy gives them feeling of uneasy for thinking about like "how he/she lives ? He/she can't hear" and this uneasy feeling gets transformed to their feelings or their treatment to that person. 

People who tend to think about their certain loved ones alot of times , makes them to cherish them or protect them or live with them , it's an builtin mechanism , when a danger occurs , people subconsciously think and focuses on their loved ones
let me summarize from grok wait

Here’s a shorter version:

The person is describing three common human patterns:

1. **Anger → regret**  
   People say or do hurtful things in anger that their calm self would never do. Later they feel deep regret.

2. **Treatment of disabled people**  
   Many look down on them because they seem “different” or “odd.”  
   Even empathetic people sometimes avoid them because imagining their life feels uncomfortable, and that unease turns into awkward or distant behavior.

3. **Protecting loved ones**  
   When danger hits, people instinctively focus on and try to shield their closest loved ones first—it’s an automatic, subconscious reaction.

In short: emotions, discomfort with difference, and protective instincts often lead to regrettable or unfair actions, even when people don’t mean to be bad.

hwelo so i am writing an short philosophical story , so i needed a lil bit feedback 🙃
Content is good, formatting and grammar needs some polishing. What is the plot of the story?

Act One — The Warmth
Winter morning. Well. Breakfast. Shoelace scene. Small ordinary moments. Reader falls completely in love with the child and mother.
Purpose: Make the reader memorize them.

Act Two — The Break
A murderer kills the child. Sudden. Cold. No dramatization — the violence should be almost quiet. Because that's how real loss arrives.
Purpose: Destroy what Act One built.

Act Three — The Manipulation
Villagers surround the grieving mother. The crowd grows loud. Someone quotes "An eye for an eye" — and the mob uses a pacifist idea to incite revenge. They convince her that killing the murderer will bring peace. She believes them because grief makes you believe anything.
Purpose: Make the reader feel manipulated alongside her.

Act Four — The Execution
She kills him herself. Blood on her hands. Crowd cheers like a festival.
Her world goes silent.
"They said I'd get peace..." — inaudible even to herself.
Purpose: The emptiness of revenge.

Act Five — The Cliff
Three weeks later. She wakes up one morning, humming. Walks to the cliff. Wind is cold.
The end.
Purpose: No resolution. Because there isn't one.

The man with big eyes proclaimed —
"An Eye For An Eye makes the whole world blind!!"
Then the man with the loud voice said —
"Yeah, he's saying right — An eye for an eye!"
Suddenly the crowd erupted. They started screaming.
"KILL HIM... KILL HIM"
"KILL HIM, TAKE YOUR REVENGE"
Men, women and children said the same.
Their combined voices rivaled thunder itself — so loud that a man standing far away would  see it.
Yet the mother's world was silent. As if all the living were dead and gone. She could only hear the sound of her own heavy heart — and nothing else.

The Crowd Scene

The man with big eyes proclaimed —
"An Eye For An Eye makes the whole world blind!!"
Then the man with the loud voice said —
"Yeah, he's saying right — An eye for an eye!"
Suddenly the crowd erupted. They started screaming.
"KILL HIM... KILL HIM"
"KILL HIM, TAKE YOUR REVENGE"
Men, women and children said the same.
Their combined voices rivaled thunder itself — so loud that a man standing far away would not hear it, but see it.
Yet the mother's world was silent. As if all the living were dead and gone. She could only hear the sound of her own heavy heart — and nothing else.


The Execution Scene (sketch)

"Justice is done" they say.
The crowd cheers. Some smile, some gossip — like a festival of joy.
The mother stands silent, emotionless on stage, blood on her hands. She pulls her hands upward, somewhat like pleading to God, staring outward. The environment is cheerful but it doesn't matter. Her world is silent — as if nobody exists in it except herself.
"They said I'd get peace....." she said, inaudible even to herself.
Her movements were sharp like a surgeon, but quite slow. If there were a person near her, all they could see would be the movement of her lips.


The Cliff Scene (sketch)

It had been three weeks since the execution. The mother was silent and heartbroken through those days — staring at her child's belongings, thinking slowly, moving slowly.
One morning she woke up lively. She hummed something old, something without a name.
Before her stood a hill, made by the earth across thousands of years. She stood on the cliff, humming. The wind was cold.
